articles.title: Telangana Weather Alert: Heavy Rains Likely in Hyderabad and Several Districts, Heatwave Red Alert Issued - Munsif News 24x7
articles.full_text:
The Hyderabad Meteorological Department has forecast light to moderate rains across Telangana today and tomorrow, bringing temporary relief to people suffering due to intense weather conditions.
Table of Contents
According to the weather department, heavy rains are likely to occur in Hyderabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Nalgonda, Vikarabad, Mahabubnagar, Kamareddy, Malkajgiri, Rangareddy, Wanaparthy and Gadwal districts.
Heavy Rain Alert Issued for Hyderabad and Other Districts
The Hyderabad Meteorological Department stated that several districts are likely to receive heavy rainfall over the next two days.
The weather update comes as residents across Telangana continue to face difficult weather conditions. The expected rainfall is likely to provide some relief from the prevailing heat in many parts of the state.
Heatwave Expected to Intensify in Telangana
Officials warned that heatwave conditions are likely to intensify in Telangana from the day after tomorrow.
A red alert has been issued for several districts in view of the expected rise in temperatures and heatwave conditions.
Authorities advised people to take precautions while stepping outside and remain alert due to the heat.
Officials Advise Public to Remain Vigilant
Officials urged people to avoid unnecessary exposure to extreme heat and follow safety precautions during the heatwave period.
Residents have been advised to stay hydrated, avoid going out during peak afternoon hours, and remain cautious as temperatures are expected to rise sharply after the rainfall period ends.
The Hyderabad Meteorological Department has predicted light to moderate rains across Telangana today and tomorrow, with heavy rainfall likely in Hyderabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Nalgonda, Vikarabad, Mahabubnagar, Kamareddy, Malkajgiri, Rangareddy, Wanaparthy and Gadwal districts. Officials also warned that heatwave conditions are expected to intensify from the day after tomorrow, and a red alert has been issued for several districts. People have been advised to take precautions and remain vigilant.
Follow MunsifNews24x7 for latest updates.
